text: Apple has unveiled a major AI overhaul, with eighty-five percent of features running on-device.
Bloomberg reports a twenty billion dollar investment, doubling last year's budget.
CEO Tim Cook called it the biggest leap since Siri.
The Electronic Frontier Foundation, however, warns cloud processing still raises privacy concerns.
Morgan Stanley predicts a twelve percent boost in iPhone sales, intensifying the AI arms race.
The takeaway is clear: tech giants now compete on privacy, not just features.
Apple's on-device strategy could force the entire industry to follow, reshaping how AI reaches one billion users.
